svg.title: Gmail by the Numbers
svg.desc: Gmail statistics sourced exclusively from Google's official blog posts and documentation. Gmail has 3 billion active users as of January 2026, up from 2.5 billion in December 2024 and 1.5 billion in April 2019. Gmail's AI-powered defenses block more than 99.9% of spam, phishing, and malware, stopping approximately 15 billion unwanted emails every day. Gmail blocks more than 100 million phishing emails daily and scans over 300 billion email attachments weekly for malware, with 63% of malicious documents being different from day to day. A new large language model deployed in 2024 blocks 20% more spam than previous models and reviews 1,000 times more user-reported spam daily. After Google required sender authentication in 2023, unauthenticated messages dropped 75%. Users reported 35% fewer scams hitting their inboxes during the 2024 holiday season compared to 2023. During a two-week holiday period in 2022, Gmail blocked 231 billion spam and phishing messages. Google requires bulk senders of 5,000 or more messages per day to maintain a spam complaint rate below 0.1% (target) and never exceed 0.3%, authenticate with SPF, DKIM, and DMARC, and support one-click unsubscribe. Non-compliant mail has been rejected with 5xx codes since November 2025.

cc:Work.dc:title: Gmail by the Numbers
cc:Work.dc:creator: InboxAlly
cc:Work.dc:subject: Gmail statistics, Gmail users, 3 billion, spam blocked, phishing blocked, 99.9 percent, 15 billion, sender requirements, bulk sender, spam rate threshold, authentication, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, email deliverability, Gmail security, AI spam filtering, LLM
cc:Work.dc:description: Official Gmail statistics organized into four tiers — scale (3B users, 15B blocked/day, 300B attachments/week), protection (99.9% block rate, 100M+ phishing/day, LLM improvements), sender requirement impact (75% auth drop, 35% fewer scams), and compliance thresholds (spam rate, authentication, unsubscribe).

g.aria-label: Tier 1: Scale — Gmail's size and growth, establishing why these numbers matter
g.data-description: Gmail platform scale: 3 billion active users, 15 billion unwanted emails blocked per day, 300 billion attachments scanned weekly, and user growth from 1.5B to 3B between 2019 and 2026

g.aria-label: 3 billion active Gmail users as of January 2026, making it the world's largest email provider
g.data-description: 3 billion active Gmail users. Source: blog.google, January 2026.

g.aria-label: Approximately 15 billion unwanted emails blocked per day including spam, phishing, and malware
g.data-description: ~15 billion unwanted emails blocked per day — spam, phishing, and malware. Source: blog.google, October 2023.
g.text: Per day — spam, phishing, and malware

g.aria-label: 300 billion email attachments scanned weekly for malware
g.data-description: 300 billion email attachments scanned per week for malware. Source: blog.google, April 2020.

g.aria-label: Gmail user growth timeline: 1.5 billion in 2019, 2.5 billion in December 2024, 3 billion in January 2026 — adding 500 million users in roughly one year
g.data-description: Gmail user growth: 1.5B (2019) → 2.5B (Dec 2024) → 3B (Jan 2026). Added 500 million users in approximately one year.

g.aria-label: Tier 2: Protection — what Gmail blocks, the filtering environment senders operate in
g.data-description: Gmail's protection capabilities: 99.9% of spam, phishing, and malware blocked; 100M+ phishing emails blocked daily; 63% of malicious documents are different day to day; new LLM blocks 20% more spam and reviews 1,000× more user-reported spam

g.aria-label: 99.9% of spam, phishing, and malware blocked before reaching inboxes
g.data-description: 99.9% of spam, phishing, and malware blocked before reaching inboxes. Confirmed across multiple Google posts 2023–2025.

g.aria-label: More than 100 million phishing emails blocked daily
g.data-description: 100M+ phishing emails blocked daily. Source: blog.google, April 2020.

g.aria-label: 63% of malicious documents are different from day to day, requiring AI-based detection
g.data-description: 63% of malicious documents are different from day to day. Source: blog.google, April 2020.

g.aria-label: New LLM deployed in 2024 blocks 20% more spam than previous models
g.data-description: 20% more spam blocked by a new large language model deployed in 2024. Source: blog.google, December 2024.

g.aria-label: New LLM reviews 1,000 times more user-reported spam daily than previous models
g.data-description: 1,000× more user-reported spam reviewed daily by the new LLM. Source: blog.google, December 2024.

g.aria-label: Tier 3: Impact of Sender Requirements — measurable results after Google imposed the 2024 bulk sender rules
g.data-description: Measurable impact of Google's sender requirements: 75% drop in unauthenticated messages, 35% fewer scams reported during 2024 holiday season vs 2023, and 231 billion spam/phishing messages blocked in two weeks during 2022 holiday season

g.aria-label: 75% drop in unauthenticated messages reaching Gmail users after authentication requirements
g.data-description: 75% drop in unauthenticated messages reaching Gmail users after Google required sender authentication. Source: blog.google, October 2023.

g.aria-label: 35% fewer scams reported by users during the 2024 holiday season compared to 2023
g.data-description: 35% fewer scams reported by users during the first month of the 2024 holiday season compared to 2023. Source: blog.google, December 2024.

g.aria-label: 231 billion spam and phishing messages blocked in just two weeks during the 2022 holiday season
g.data-description: 231 billion spam and phishing messages blocked in a two-week holiday period. Source: blog.google, December 2022.

g.aria-label: Tier 4: Sender Thresholds — what bulk senders must comply with to reach Gmail inboxes
g.data-description: Google's bulk sender requirements: 5,000+ messages/day threshold, spam complaint rate below 0.1% target and below 0.3% maximum, SPF + DKIM + DMARC authentication required, one-click unsubscribe required. Soft enforcement from February 2024, strict 5xx rejection from November 2025.

g.aria-label: Sources: all data sourced from Google's official blog posts and Workspace Admin documentation
g.data-description: All statistics sourced exclusively from blog.google posts and Google Workspace Admin Help pages. Zero third-party data.
